goal: Fix website English and Spanish copy typos

summary.title: Fix website English and Spanish copy typos
summary.what: Corrected localized website copy issues in Spanish pages and centralized copy: accents, inverted question marks, spacing, title capitalization, and interrogative wording. Confirmed the reported English histórical typo is already absent on current main.
summary.why: The website should not ship visible typo and grammar defects in meta text, headings, localized SEO pages, or the Spanish essay.
summary.user_goal: Fix the English historical typo if present and clean up Spanish accent and grammar issues on a new branch.

decisions.chose: Limit this pass to obvious typo, accent, punctuation, spacing, and grammar corrections.
decisions.rationale: The inherited localization risk asks for native-speaker review, but this task was a targeted bugfix and should not rewrite the Spanish positioning voice wholesale.

decisions.chose: Preserve established product terms such as coding agents, repo memory, intent, review, and Git-native.
decisions.rationale: Those terms are used consistently across the localized site; the corrections only repaired Spanish grammar around them.

rejected.reason: That would be broader than the requested typo and grammar cleanup and should be handled as native-speaker review if needed.

review_notes: This reduces obvious defects but does not close the broader int_3e4b3ed4#0 native-speaker review risk for all Spanish and Chinese copy.

behavioral_changes: Spanish localized homepage, blog listing, SEO page, AI PR review copy, and Spanish repo-memory essay render corrected typo, accent, capitalization, and question punctuation text.
English historical copy remains spelled without the reported accent typo on current main.
